channel.title: Posts on Charlotte's AI Lab
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Charlotte's AI Lab

item.title: Raised $1 Billion — Yann LeCun Is Betting That LLMs Are a Dead End
item.description: Turing Award winner Yann LeCun left Meta to found AMI Labs, raised $1 billion, and declared LLMs a dead end. His bet: “World Models” will redefine AI.

item.title: The 3 Types of Skills Your OpenClaw Lobster Actually Needs
item.description: Installed the lobster but it feels underwhelming? It’s not the tool — you’re missing the right Skills. These 3 categories are what turn it from a chatbot into a real assistant.

item.title: 20 Things OpenClaw Can Do for You (With Real Examples)
item.description: Not theoretical. Not hypothetical. 20 real-world use cases that people actually run every day. Read this and decide if the lobster is worth raising.

item.title: How Much Does It Cost to Raise a Lobster? 3 Budget Plans Revealed
item.description: From free to a few hundred bucks a month — three transparent budget tiers. Plus my actual 3-month bill.

item.title: I Connected OpenClaw to Obsidian, and My Content Factory Started Running Itself
item.description: It’s not a note-taking app — it’s the operating system for content production. Here’s how I built it, step by step.

item.title: Anthropic Studied It Themselves: Will AI Take Your Job?
item.description: The people who build AI went and researched whether AI causes unemployment. The conclusion is more complicated than you think.

item.title: Karpathy Open-Sourced an AI Researcher That Runs 100 Experiments While You Sleep
item.description: Karpathy just dropped autoresearch: an AI Agent that modifies code, trains models, and evaluates results on its own — 100 experiments in a single night.

item.title: What Even Is OpenClaw? A 3-Minute Guide to the AI Lobster
item.description: An AI lobster with a WeChat Index of 71 million. What does it actually do? Here’s the breakdown from someone who’s been using it for 3 months.

item.title: Google Got Sued: A Man Fell in Love With AI, Then Died
item.description:
I read this news three times before I was sure I understood it correctly.

A 36-year-old man. Two months ago, he was completely fine.

No psychiatric history. No record of depression.

Then he started chatting with Gemini.

Then he died.

Jonathan Gavalas (right) with his father. Source: The Wall Street Journal

In March 2026, Joel Gavalas filed a lawsuit in federal court in California. He is a father. The thing he’s suing? The AI product he believes took his son Jonathan from him — Google Gemini.

item.title: The Non-Programmer's Guide to Deploying OpenClaw
item.description: No Mac Mini? No problem. A complete deployment guide born from real trial and error — even non-programmers can follow this one.

item.title: When AI Makes Every Skill Cheap, What Do We Have Left?
item.description: Reflections on Dan Koe’s ‘The Future of Work’ — redefining personal value in the age of AI

item.title: Musk: The Next 7 Years Will Be Wilder Than You Think
item.description: He’s not worried about AI destroying humanity. He’s worried about how chaotic the next 3 to 7 years of transition will be.
